summary: Validated the initial M3 H-003 and H-005 evidence without changing product scope. Added a dedicated validation test that proves three postwar governance methods produce different 24-month outcomes from the M3 fixture and that reason/proposal cues are useful but not always optimal. The browser smoke still covers appointment submission, bulk eligible submission, per-item rejection visibility, and reason display.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec vitest run tests/m3-h003-h005-validation.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts
tests_run.result: 1 file passed; 2 tests passed. H-003 comparison uses seed 1531 and contentManifestHash content.m3.postwar.validation. H-005 reason coverage uses the client appointment fixture and a separate appointment world.

decisions.finding: H-003 evidence is fixture-level and deterministic, not a global balance claim. The three governance methods are intentionally different and the direct-control option is not trivially dominant when administrative load, reliability, troop contribution, tribute, and risk are all considered.

H-005 evidence is measurable but still fixture-level. A stronger-skill candidate can lose to a supportive/military candidate, and the client read model exposes reason-code coverage across appointment, behavior, bulk, enfeoffment, obligation, policy, and succession sources.

Gameplay_designer and historical_researcher both posted PARTIAL outbox evidence during this turn. Their reads were useful for scope confirmation and did not require any forbidden-path edits or Human Gate escalation.

risks: H-005 still does not measure live player acceptance or regret rates; it only proves measurable non-optimality inside the validation fixture.
The browser smoke is protocol/read-model coverage, not a live production worker integration proof.
Two concurrent PARTIAL outbox files from other roles remain unmodified in the workspace.
requested_action: Review the validation evidence and accept if the initial H-003/H-005 evidence level is sufficient. If you require live proposal telemetry instead of fixture evidence, request a narrowly scoped follow-up.
